findings.issue: Debug eprintln! fires on every OpenAI streaming chunk in production, polluting stderr
findings.recommendation: Remove the eprintln! block (lines 411-415). The comment even says 'DEBUG: temporarily log'. Use tracing::trace! if diagnostics are needed.

findings.issue: Three debug eprint!/eprintln! calls stream raw review agent output directly to stderr in production code
findings.recommendation: Remove eprint!("{t}"), eprintln!("[tool: {}]", tc.name), and the trailing eprintln!(). Route output through the tracing framework (debug!/trace!) or return it via the AgentEvent stream instead of bypassing it.

findings.issue: .expect() panic inside a spawned tokio task — panics the async task instead of returning an error through the event channel
findings.code_snippet: shared_stack.expect("shared stack exists for non-isolated background runs")

findings.issue: std::sync::RwLock::write().expect("PlanState poisoned") — lock poison propagates panics from other threads to all future callers
findings.recommendation: Handle poison gracefully using .unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner()) to recover the inner value, or switch to tokio::sync::RwLock which does not poison. Apply to all three call sites (set, get, clear).

findings.issue: std::sync::RwLock lock poison propagation in TodoState — same pattern as PlanState
findings.recommendation: Use .unwrap_or_else(|poisoned| poisoned.into_inner()) for all .read()/.write() calls in TodoState, or switch to tokio::sync::RwLock.

findings.issue: OnceLock tiktoken BPE initialization uses .expect() — panics at first token count call if tiktoken data is unavailable
findings.recommendation: Propagate initialization failure gracefully: make get_cl100k() and get_o200k() return Result<&'static CoreBPE, ...>, and have count_tokens() return Result<usize, ...> or fall back to a character-based estimate. This prevents hard crashes in environments where tiktoken data may not be embedded correctly.

findings.issue: .expect() on compile-time embedded JSON parse — startup panic if registry.json is malformed at compile time (caught late, not at compile time)
findings.recommendation: Add a compile-time test (e.g. #[test] fn registry_json_parses()) to catch malformed registry.json at test time rather than at runtime. Consider using a build.rs assertion. The current pattern is acceptable but fragile during active development of registry.json.
findings.code_snippet:
serde_json::from_str(REGISTRY_JSON)
    .expect("registry.json is embedded at compile time and must be valid")

findings.issue: .expect() in the main agent run path — relies on a non-local invariant (injected_provider.is_none() iff route_decision.is_some())
findings.recommendation: Make the invariant explicit using a match or if-let on both fields together to avoid logic errors if the initialization code (lines 132-136) is ever refactored. A single enum RouteStrategy { Injected(Arc<dyn ...>), Routed(RouteDecision) } would eliminate this class of bug entirely.

findings.issue: Custom Outcome<T,E> type defines an unwrap() that panics, shadowing the standard Result convention without a trait
findings.recommendation: Implement std::ops::Try or at minimum document that Outcome::unwrap() panics like Option/Result. Alternatively, return the standard Result<T,E> type from benchmark harness functions to leverage the existing ? operator ecosystem.

findings.issue: Comment 'DEBUG: temporarily log' indicates the eprintln! was never meant to ship — suggests weak code review or CI checks for debug output
findings.recommendation: Add a clippy lint or CI check for eprintln!/eprint! in non-TUI crates to prevent debug prints from shipping. A simple grep in CI suffices: grep -rn 'eprintln!\|eprint!' crates/ --include='*.rs' | grep -v 'ava-tui'
findings.code_snippet: // DEBUG: temporarily log reasoning_content presence for diagnostics

findings.issue: PlanState wraps Arc<RwLock<...>> but is itself Clone and Default, creating subtle shared-state semantics: cloning PlanState shares the inner Arc, not a copy of the plan
findings.recommendation: Remove the #[derive(Clone)] from PlanState or document the shared-reference semantics explicitly with a doc comment. If copy semantics are needed, clone the inner Option<Plan> directly. Misleading Clone impls cause hard-to-debug state sharing bugs.

findings.issue: spawn_background_task function signature accepts Option<Arc<AgentStack>> for shared_stack but documents no precondition about when None is valid
findings.recommendation: Add a doc comment explaining the precondition: None is only valid when isolated_branch=true. Consider making this a type-level constraint: use an enum BackgroundStackMode { Isolated, Shared(Arc<AgentStack>) } to eliminate the Option + bool combination and make the invariant compile-time checked.
findings.code_snippet:
// Precondition is currently enforced only by .expect() at line 163
// Option<Arc<AgentStack>> + bool isolated_branch are logically coupled
